Name: Chespin
Flavor-Text: The quills on its head are usually soft. When it flexes them, the points become so hard and sharp that they can pierce rock.

Name: Fennekin
Flavor-Text: Eating a twig fills it with energy, and its roomy ears give vent to air hotter than 390 degrees Fahrenheit.

Name: Froakie
Flavor-Text: It secretes flexible bubbles from its chest and back. The bubbles reduce the damage it would otherwise take when attacked.

Name: Sylveon
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: It sends a soothing aura from its ribbonlike feelers to calm fights.

Name: Xerneas
Effects: Choose 2 of your Benched Pokémon. For each of those Pokémon, search your deck for a [Y] Energy card and attach it to that Pokémon. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Legends say it can share eternal life. It slept for a thousand years in the form of a tree before its revival.

Name: Yveltal
Effects: Attach a [D] Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flip a coin. If tails, this Pokémon can’t attack during your next turn.
Flavor-Text: When this legendary Pokémon’s wings and tail feathers spread wide and glow red, it absorbs the life force of all living creatures.

Name: Xerneas-EX
Effects: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
This Pokémon can’t use X Blast during your next turn.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Yveltal-EX
Effects: This attack does 20 more damage times the amount of Energy attached to both Active Pokémon.
Move an Energy from this Pokémon to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Garchomp-EX
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.
Discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Dragalge
Ability-Effect: Your opponent’s Poisoned Pokémon can’t retreat.
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: Their poison is strong enough to eat through the hull of a tanker, and they spit it indiscriminately at anything that enters their territory.

Name: Skiddo
Effects: This Pokémon does 10 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: Thought to be one of the first Pokémon to live in harmony with humans, it has a placid disposition.

Name: Honedge
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon’s Slash attack’s base damage is 40.
Flavor-Text: Apparently this Pokémon is born when a departed spirit inhabits a sword. It attaches itself to people and drinks their life force.

Name: Charizard-EX
Effects: Search your deck for M Charizard-EX, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
This Pokémon does 30 damage to itself.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Machamp
Ability-Effect: Each of your [F] Pokémon’s attacks do 20 more damage to your opponent’s Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, any damage done by attacks from the Defending Pokémon is reduced by 40 (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: Its four ruggedly developed arms can launch a flurry of 1,000 punches in just two seconds.

Name: Chesnaught-EX
Effects: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 40 damage times the number of heads.
This Pokémon does 20 damage to itself.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Delphox-EX
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Your opponent reveals his or her hand. This attack does 40 more damage for each Energy card in your opponent’s hand.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Greninja-EX
Effects: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Discard 1 [W] Energy attached to this Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Krookodile-EX
Effects: This attack does 10 more damage for each damage counter on your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Discard a card from your hand. If you can’t discard a card, this attack does nothing.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Pyroar
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy attached to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Discard 2 Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The male with the largest mane of fire is the leader of the pride.

Name: Slurpuff
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, any damage done to this Pokémon by attacks is reduced by 30 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: It can distinguish the faintest of scents. It puts its sensitive sense of smell to use by helping pastry chefs in their work.

Name: Champions Festival
Description: Once during each player’s turn, if that player has 6 Pokémon in play, he or she may heal 10 damage from each of his or her Pokémon.
Stadium-Rule: This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. If another card with the same name is in play, you can’t play this card.

Name: Trevenant
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Flip a coin. If tails, this Pokémon does 20 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: It can control trees at will. It will trap people who harm the forest, so they can never leave.

Name: Gogoat
Effects: Your opponent switches his or her Active Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.
Flip a coin for each [G] Energy attached to this Pokémon. This attack does 30 more damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: It can tell how its trainer is feeling by subtle shifts in the grip on its horns. This empathetic sense lets them run as if one being.

Name: Bronzong
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may attach a [M] Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: In ages past, this Pokémon was revered as a bringer of rain. It was found buried in the ground.

Name: Darkrai
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is Asleep, this attack does 60 more damage and heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It chases people and Pokémon from its territory by causing them to experience deep, nightmarish slumbers.

Name: Shiftry
Effects: Your opponent reveals his or her hand. Choose a Pokémon you find there and put it on the bottom your opponent’s deck. If you do, this attack does 30 more damage.
Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 more damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: A Pokémon that was feared as a forest guardian. It can read the foe’s mind and take preemptive action.

Name: Greninja
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon, you may prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon by your opponent’s Pokémon during your opponent’s next turn.
Effects: This attack does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: It creates throwing stars out of compressed water. When it spins them and throws them at high speed, these stars can split metal in two.

Name: Venusaur-EX
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Charizard-EX
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, search your deck for up to 3 basic Energy cards and attach them to this Pokémon. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Blastoise-EX
Effects: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon. Then, your opponent switches his or her Active Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.
Flip a coin. If tails, this Pokémon does 30 damage to itself.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Xerneas
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, this Pokémon has no Weakness.
If your opponent has Yveltal (including Yveltal-EX) in play, this attack does 40 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Legends say it can share eternal life. It slept for a thousand years in the form of a tree before its revival.

Name: Yveltal
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy attached to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
If your opponent has Xerneas (including Xerneas-EX) in play, this attack does 40 more damage.
Flavor-Text: When this legendary Pokémon’s wings and tail feathers spread wide and glow red, it absorbs the life force of living creatures.

Name: Trevor
Description: Search your deck for a Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Metagross-EX
Effects: You may move a [M] Energy from 1 of your Benched Pokémon to this Pokémon.
Flip 4 coins. This attack does 50 damage times the number of heads.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: M Metagross-EX
Effects: This attack does 10 more damage for each [M] Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Mega-Evolution-Rule: When 1 of your Pokémon becomes a Mega Evolution Pokémon, your turn ends.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Treecko
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Small hooks on the bottom of its feet catch on walls and ceilings. That is how it can hang from above.

Name: Torchic
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, discard a [R] Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: A fire burns inside, so it feels very warm to hug. It launches fireballs of 1,800 degrees Fahrenheit.

Name: Mudkip
Flavor-Text: To alert it, the fin on its head senses the flow of water. It has the strength to heft boulders.

Name: Kingdra
Effects: Search your deck for up to 4 basic Energy cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Discard a [W] and a [L] Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Ancient-Trait-Effect: When you attach an Energy card from your hand to this Pokémon (except with an attack, Ability, or Trainer card), you may attach 2 Energy cards.
Flavor-Text: It stores energy by sleeping at underwater depths at which no other life-forms can survive.

Name: Ditto
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon, it can use the attacks of your opponent’s Active Pokémon. (You still need the necessary Energy to use each attack.)
Effects: Attach a basic Energy card from your discard pile to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It has the ability to reconstitute its entire cellular structure to transform into whatever it sees.

Name: Kyogre-EX
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
Return 2 [W] Energy attached to this Pokémon to your hand.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Groudon-EX
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy attached to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, any damage done by attacks from the Defending Pokémon is reduced by 20 (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flip 2 coins. This attack does 40 more damage for each heads.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Effects: If your opponent’s Active Pokémon has no Energy attached to it, this attack does 40 more damage.
This Pokémon can’t use Cross Slash during your next turn.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Kyogre
Effects: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.
This attack does 10 damage to each of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: It is said to have widened the seas by causing downpours. It had been asleep in a marine trench.

Name: Groudon
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.
This attack does 10 damage to each of your Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: This legendary Pokémon is said to represent the land. It went to sleep after dueling Kyogre.

Name: Altaria
Ability-Effect: Each of your [C] Pokémon has no Weakness.
Ancient-Trait-Effect: You may play this card from your hand to evolve a Pokémon during your first turn or the turn you play that Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: On sunny days, it flies freely through the sky and blends into the clouds. It sings in a beautiful soprano.

Name: Heliolisk
Ability-Effect: Any damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from your opponent’s [W] Pokémon is reduced by 30 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy attached to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: They flare their frills and generate energy. A single Heliolisk can generate sufficient electricity to power a skyscraper.

Name: Meowstic
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon and is damaged by an opponent’s attack (even if this Pokémon is Knocked Out), the Attacking Pokémon is now Confused.
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy attached to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: When in danger, it raises its ears and releases enough psychic power to grind a 10-ton truck into dust.

Name: Regirock
Effects: Put 2 Stadium cards from your discard pile into your hand.
Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 40 more damage.
Ancient-Trait-Effect: Whenever your opponent plays a Trainer card (excluding Pokémon Tools and Stadium cards), prevent all effects of that card done to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The same rocks that form its body have been found in ground layers around the world.

Name: Pangoro
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon, it gets +20 HP for each of your Benched Pokémon.
Effects: If this Pokémon is affected by a Special Condition, this attack does 40 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Although it possesses a violent temperament, it won’t put up with bullying. It uses the leaf in its mouth to sense the movements of its enemies.

Name: Swampert-EX

This Pokémon does 20 damage to itself.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Sceptile-EX
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon during your opponent’s next turn.

ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Flip a coin for each [R] Energy attached to this Pokémon. This attack does 20 more damage for each heads.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Blissey
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 40 damage times the number of heads.
Heal 100 damage from each of your Benched Pokémon. Then, discard 2 Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The eggs it lays are filled with happiness. Eating even one bite will bring a smile to anyone.

Name: Fearow
Effects: Flip 5 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.
This Pokémon does 20 damage to itself.
Ancient-Trait-Effect: You may play this card from your hand to evolve a Pokémon during your first turn or the turn you play that Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It has the stamina to fly all day on its broad wings. It fights by using its sharp beak.

Name: Malamar
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon, whenever your opponent flips a coin during his or her turn, treat it as tails.
Effects: If you have the same number of cards in your hand as your opponent, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Ancient-Trait-Effect: Prevent all effects of your opponent’s Pokémon’s Abilities done to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It lures its prey close with hypnotic motions, then wraps its tentacles around it before finishing it off with digestive fluids.

Name: Salamence
Effects: Search your deck for up to 3 Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Discard the top card of your deck.
Ancient-Trait-Effect: You may play this card from your hand to evolve a Pokémon during your first turn or the turn you play that Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It’s uncontrollable if enraged. It flies around spouting flames and scorching fields and mountains.

Name: Gyarados
Effects: This attack does 30 more damage for each damage counter on each of your Benched Magikarp.
Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage. If tails, this Pokémon does 30 damage to itself.
Ancient-Trait-Effect: This Pokémon may have up to 2 Pokémon Tool cards attached to it.
Flavor-Text: Rarely seen in the wild. Huge and vicious, it is capable of destroying entire cities in a rage.

ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Rayquaza-EX
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this Pokémon can’t use Dragon Strike during your next turn.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Jirachi

Flavor-Text: It is said to have the ability to grant any wish for just one week every thousand years.

Name: Chesnaught
Effects: If your opponent’s Active Pokémon already has any damage counters on it, this attack does 60 more damage.
During your opponent’s next turn, any damage done to this Pokémon by attacks is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: When it takes a defensive posture with its fists guarding its face, it could withstand a bomb blast.

Name: Reshiram
Effects: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 20 more damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: When Reshiram’s tail flares, the heat energy moves the atmosphere and changes the world’s weather.

Name: Palkia
Effects: Your opponent can’t attach Energy from his or her hand to the Defending Pokémon during his or her next turn.
Flavor-Text: It has the ability to distort space. It is described as a deity in Sinnoh-region mythology.

Name: Zekrom
Effects: This attack does 10 damage to each of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: Concealing itself in lightning clouds, it flies throughout the Unova region. It creates electricity in its tail.

Name: Dialga
Effects: Your opponent can’t play any Pokémon from his or her hand to evolve the Defending Pokémon during his or her next turn.
Flavor-Text: It has the power to control time. It appears in Sinnoh-region myths as an ancient deity.

Name: Latias

Name: Latios
Effects: If Latias is on your Bench, this attack does 50 more damage.

Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
This Pokémon does 20 damage to itself.

Flavor-Text: Using its roots as a nervous system, it controls the trees in the forest. It’s kind to the Pokémon that reside in its body.

Name: Pikachu
Effects: Heal 30 damage from both Active Pokémon.
Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It has small electric sacs on both its cheeks. If threatened, it looses electric charges from the sacs.

Name: Umbreon
Effects: This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Resistance.
Turn 1 of your face-down Prize cards face up. If that Prize card is a Pokémon, this attack does 60 more damage. (That Prize card remains up for the rest of the game.)
Flavor-Text: When exposed to the moon’s aura, the rings on its body glow faintly and it’s filled with a mysterious power.

Name: Gyarados
Effects: This attack does 10 damage to each Benched Pokémon (both yours and your opponent’s). (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flip a coin for each [W] Energy attached to this Pokémon. This attack does 30 more damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: Rarely seen in the wild. Huge and vicious, it is capable of destroying entire cities in a rage.

Name: Mew
Effects: Your opponent reveals his or her hand.
This attack does 10 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Its DNA is said to contain the genetic codes of all Pokémon, so it can use all kinds of techniques.

Name: Magikarp
Effects: Flip 2 coins. If either of them is tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: In the distant past, it was somewhat stronger than the horribly weak descendants that exist today.

Name: Celebi
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, search your deck for a card that evolves from 1 of your Pokémon and put it onto that Pokémon. (This counts as evolving that Pokémon.) Shuffle your deck afterward.
Heal 10 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It has the power to travel across time, but it is said to appear only in peaceful times.

Name: Charizard-EX
Effects: This Pokémon can’t use Combustion Blast during your next turn.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Gyarados-EX
Effects: Flips a coin until you get tails. For each heads, search your deck for a [W] Energy card and attach it to this Pokémon. Shuffle your deck afterward.
This attack does 10 damage to each of your Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Mewtwo-EX
Effects: This attack does 30 damage times the amount of [P] Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Switch all damage counters on this Pokémon with those on your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Machamp-EX
Effects: This attack does 20 damage times the number of damage counters on this Pokémon. This Pokémon is now Confused.
If this Pokémon is affected by a Special Condition, this attack does 80 more damage. Then, remove all Special Conditions from this Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Jirachi
Effects: Draw 3 cards. This Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: It is said to have the ability to grant any wish for just one week every thousand years.

Name: Ash-Greninja-EX
Effects: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
This Pokémon can’t use Ninja Blade during your next turn.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Moltres
Effects: You may do 40 more damage. If you do, this Pokémon does 20 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: One of the legendary bird Pokémon. It is said that its appearance indicates the coming of spring.

Name: White Kyurem
Effects: If this Pokémon has any [R] Energy attached to it, this attack does 20 damage to 2 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
This Pokémon can’t attack during your next turn.
Flavor-Text: It generates a powerful, freezing energy inside itself, but its body becomes frozen when the energy leaked out.

Name: Zygarde
Effects: The Defending Pokémon can’t retreat during your opponent’s next turn.
This attack does 10 damage to each of your Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: It’s hypothesized that it’s monitoring those those who destroy the ecosystem from deep in the cave where it lives.

Name: Tyranitar
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon, you may attach 1 [D] Energy from your discard pile to this Pokémon for each Prize card your opponent has taken.
Effects: Discard the top 2 cards of your deck. This attack does 50 more damage for each Supporter card discard in this way.
Flavor-Text: If it ramages, it knocks down mountains and buries rivers. Maps must be redrawn afterward.

Name: Darkrai
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Asleep. Your opponent flips 2 coins instead of 1 between turns. If either of them is tails, that Pokémon is still Asleep.
Flavor-Text: It can lull people to sleep and make them dream. It is active during nights of the new moon.

Name: Mawile-EX
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy attached to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: M Mawile-EX
Effects: Flip 2 coins. For each heads, discard an Energy attached to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Mega-Evolution-Rule: When 1 of your Pokémon becomes a Mega Evolution Pokémon, your turn ends.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Mawile Spirit Link
Description: Your turn does not end if the Pokémon this card is attached to becomes M Mawile-EX.
Tool-Rule: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn’t already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.

Name: Pansear
Effects: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon lives in caves with volcanoes. The fire within the tuft on its head can reach 600 degrees Fahrenheit.

Name: Gastly
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Asleep and Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: Born from gases, anyone would faint if engulfed by its gaseous body, which contains poison.

Name: Vivillon
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused and Poisoned.
This attack does 30 more damage for each [R] Energy attached to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The patterns on this Pokémon’s wings depend on the climate and topography of its habitat. It scatters colorful scales.

Name: Froakie
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: It protects its skin by covering its body in delicate bubbles. Beneath its happy-go-lucky air, it keeps a watchful eye on its surroundings.

Name: Xerneas-EX
Effects: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
This Pokémon can’t use X Blast during your next turn.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Yveltal-EX
Effects: This attack does 20 more damage times the amount of Energy attached to both Active Pokémon.
Move an Energy from this Pokémon to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Zygarde-EX
Effects: If there is any Stadium card in play, this attack does 20 more damage.
Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Blastoise-EX
Effects: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon. Then, your opponent switches his or her Active Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.
Flip a coin. If heads, this Pokémon does 30 damage to itself.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Manaphy
Effects: Search your deck for a [W] Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It starts its life with a wondrous power that permits it to bond with any kind of Pokémon.

Name: Zygarde
Effects: The Defending Pokémon can’t retreat during your opponent’s next turn.
This attack does 10 damage to each of your Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: It’s hypothesized that it’s monitoring those who destroy the ecosystem from deep in the cave where it lives.

Name: Shaymin
Effects: Heal 30 damage from each of your Pokémon.
Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage and heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The blooming of Gracidea flowers confers the power of flight upon it. Feelings of gratitude are the message it delivers.

Name: Shaymin-EX
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may heal 20 damage from each of your Benched Basic Pokémon.
Effects: Heal 20 damage and remove all Special Conditions from this Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Venusaur-EX
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Ho-Oh
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, search your deck for up to 2 [R] Energy cards and attach them to this Pokémon. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Flavor-Text: It will reveal itself before a pure-hearted Trainer by shining its bright, rainbow-colored wings.

Name: Ho-Oh BREAK
Effects: This Pokémon can’t use Shining Flame during your next turn.
BREAK-Rule: Ho-Oh BREAK retains the attacks, Abilities, Weakness, Resistance, and Retreat Cost of its previous Evolution.

Name: Wobbuffet BREAK
Effects: Discard all Energy attached to this Pokémon. During your opponent’s next turn, if this Pokémon is damaged by an attack (even if this Pokémon is Knocked Out), put damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon equal to the damage done to this Pokémon.
BREAK-Rule: Wobbuffet BREAK retains the attacks, Abilities, Weakness, Resistance, and Retreat Cost of its previous Evolution.

Name: Lugia
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 more damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: It is said to be the guardian of the seas. It is rumored to have been seen on the night of a storm.

Name: Arceus
Effects: Choose [G], [R], [W], [L], [P], [F], [D], [M], [Y], or [N] type. Until the ends of your next turn, this Pokémon is that type.
Discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It is told in mythology that this Pokémon was born before the universe even existed.

Name: Floette
Effects: Heal 30 damage from each of your Pokémon.
Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage and heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: When the flowers of a well-tended flower bed bloom, it appears and celebrates with an elegant dance.

Name: Lucario
Effects: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.
This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Resistance.
Flavor-Text: By reading the auras of all things, it can tell how others are feeling from over half a mile away.

Name: Rayquaza
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.
Discard 1 [R] Energy and 1 [L] Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It flies in the ozone layer, way up high in the sky. Until recently, no one had ever seen it.

Name: Azelf
Effects: Put 2 damage counters on each of your opponent’s Pokémon that has any damage counters on it.
Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: It is thought that Uxie, Mesprit, and Azelf all came from the same egg.

Name: Yanmega
Ability-Effect: If you have exactly 4 cards in your hand, ignore all Energy in the attack cost of each of this Pokémon’s attacks.
Effects: If your opponent’s Active Pokémon has a Pokémon Tool card attached to it, this attack does 70 more damage.
Flavor-Text: This six-legged Pokémon is easily capable of transporting an adult in flight. The wings on its tail help it stay balanced.

Name: Volcanion
Effects: Choose 2 of your Benched Pokémon. Attach a [R] Energy card from your discard pile to each of those Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It expels its internal steam from the arms on its back. It has enough power to blow away a mountain.

Name: Clawitzer
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may attach a Special Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Mega Evolution Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: By expelling water from the nozzle in the back of its claw, it can move at a speed of 60 knots.

Name: Hoopa
Effects: This attack does 20 damage to 2 of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
This Pokémon can’t use Portal Strike during your next turn.
Flavor-Text: It is said to be able to seize anything it desires with its six rings and six huge arms. With its power sealed, it is transformed into a much smaller form.

Name: Champions Festival
Description: Once during each player’s turn, if that player has 6 Pokémon in play, he or she may heal 10 damage from each of his or her Pokémon.
Stadium-Rule: This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. If another card with the same name is in play, you can’t play this card.

Name: Victini
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, attach a basic Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Discard a [R] Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: When it shares the infinite energy it creates, that being’s entire body will be overflowing with power.

Name: Pikachu-EX
Effects: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.
Discard all [L] Energy attached to this Pokémon. This attack does 50 damage times the number of Energy cards you discarded.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Mewtwo-EX
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, any damage done by attacks from the Defending Pokémon is reduced by 30 (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Mew-EX
Ability-Effect: This Pokémon can use the attacks of any Pokémon in play (both yours and your opponent’s). (You still need the necessary Energy to use each attack.)
Effects: Move as many Energy attached to your Pokémon to your other Pokémon in any way you like.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Karen
Description: Each player shuffles all Pokémon in his or her discard pile into his or her deck.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Volcanion-EX
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may discard a [R] Energy card from your hand. If you do, during this turn, your Basic [R] Pokémon’s attacks do 30 more damage to your opponent’s Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Effects: This Pokémon can’t attack during your next turn.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Pikachu-EX
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Discard all Energy attached to this Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Magearna-EX
Ability-Effect: Prevent all effects of your opponent’s attacks, except damage, done to each of your Pokémon that has any [M] Energy attached to it. (Existing effects are not removed.)
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon’s Soul Blaster attack’s base damage is 60.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Keldeo
Ability-Effect: This Pokémon’s attacks do 50 more damage to your opponent’s [D] Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Effects: This Pokémon can’t use Sacred Sword during your next turn.
Flavor-Text: When it is resolute, its body fills with power and it becomes swifter. Its jumps are too fast to follow.

Name: Mewtwo-EX
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, any damage done by attacks from the Defending Pokémon is reduced by 30 (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Beedrill-EX
Effects: Discard up to 2 Pokémon Tool cards attached to your opponent’s Pokémon.
Flip 4 coins. This attack does 40 damage times the number of heads.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: M Beedrill-EX
Effects: Discard all Energy attached to this Pokémon. Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed and Poisoned. Put 4 damage counters instead of 1 on that Pokémon between turns.
Mega-Evolution-Rule: When 1 of your Pokémon becomes a Mega Evolution Pokémon, your turn ends.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Description: Your turn does not end if the Pokémon this card is attached to becomes M Beedrill-EX.

Effects: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy attached to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon existed 300 million years ago. Team Plasma altered it and attached a cannon to its back.

Name: Black Kyurem
Effects: If this Pokémon has any [L] Energy attached to it, this attack does 20 damage to each of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: It generates a powerful, freezing energy inside itself, but its body became frozen when the energy leaked out.

Name: Braixen
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy attached to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: When the twig is plucked from its tail, friction sets the twig alight. The flame is used to send signals to its allies.

Name: Greninja
Effects: This attack does 20 damage to each of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flip 2 coins. This attack does 60 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: It appears and vanishes with a ninja’s grace. It toys with its enemies using swift movements, while slicing them with throwing stars of sharpest water.

Name: Weezing
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon has any Energy attached to it, this Pokémon has no Retreat Cost.
Effects: If the Defending Pokémon tries to attack during your opponent’s next turn, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: Very rarely, a sudden mutation can result in two small Koffing twins becoming conjoined as a Weezing.

Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused and Poisoned.

Effects: The Defending Pokémon can’t retreat during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: It appears when it senses an impending natural disaster. As a result, it was mistaken as a doom bringer.

Effects: Search your deck for a [L] Energy card and attach it to this Pokémon. Shuffle your deck afterward.

Name: M Sharpedo-EX
Effects: This attack does 10 damage to each of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon for each [C] in that Pokémon’s Retreat Cost. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Mega-Evolution-Rule: When 1 of your Pokémon becomes a Mega Evolution Pokémon, your turn ends.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Sharpedo Spirit Link
Description: Your turn does not end if the Pokémon this card is attached to becomes M Sharpedo-EX.
Tool-Rule: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn’t already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.

Name: Lucario Spirit Link
Description: Your turn does not end if the Pokémon this card is attached to becomes M Lucario-EX.
Tool-Rule: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn’t already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.

Name: Jirachi
Effects: Discard a Special Energy attached to your opponent’s Active Pokémon. If you do, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon during your opponent’s next turn.
Both Active Pokémon are now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: It is said to have the ability to grant any wish for just one week every thousand years.

Name: Yveltal-EX
Effects: This attack does 20 more damage times the amount of Energy attached to both Active Pokémon.
Move an Energy from this Pokémon to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Karen
Description: Each player shuffles all Pokémon in his or her discard pile into his or her deck.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Name: M Camerupt-EX
Effects: You may discard the top 3 cards of each player’s deck. If you do, this attack does 40 more damage for each Energy card you discarded in this way.
Mega-Evolution-Rule: When 1 of your Pokémon becomes a Mega Evolution Pokémon, your turn ends.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.
